factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting with a notable increase in service revenues relative to product sales, particularly in higher-margin segments such as Energy and Fleet Support services. However, this shift has led to a decrease in segment operating income percentages, indicating a potential trade-off between revenue growth and margin performance.

evidence.text: Service revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2016, increased $17 million, or 5%, compared with the same period in 2015. Product sales decreased $134 million, or 9%, during the same period.
Segment operating income as a percentage of sales and service revenues decreased from 13.0% in 2015 to 11.4% in 2016 for Ingalls and from 8.5% in 2015 to 7.4% in 2016 for Newport News.

Higher Energy revenues were primarily due to higher volumes and the resolution of outstanding contract changes on a commercial contract, partially offset by lower volumes associated with environmental remediation programs. Fleet Support services revenues were higher primarily due to higher volumes in Aircraft Carrier fleet support services.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with some segments showing growth and others experiencing decline. Product sales have seen a decrease, while service revenues have increased. Overall, there is a noticeable deceleration in demand based on revenue trends.

evidence.text: Product sales for the three months ended September 30, 2016, decreased $134 million, or 9%, compared with the same period in 2015. Service revenues increased $17 million, or 5%, compared with the same period in 2015.

Sales and service revenues decreased $117 million, or 7%, for the three months ended September 30, 2016, compared with the same period in 2015. The decrease was primarily due to lower revenues in Amphibious Assault Ships and the Legend-class NSC program, partially offset by higher revenues in Surface Combatants.

Newport News revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2016, decreased $105 million, or 9%, primarily driven by lower revenues in Aircraft Carriers and Submarines. ... Higher Energy revenues were primarily due to higher volumes and the resolution of outstanding contract changes on a commercial contract, partially offset by lower volumes associated with environmental remediation programs.

factors.summary: Organic growth is decelerating, with notable declines in product sales and mixed performance across services. evidence.text: Product sales decreased 9% year-over-year, while service revenues increased 5%. Overall sales and service revenues decreased 7% year-over-year.
Sales and service revenues decreased $117 million, or 7%, for the three months ended September 30, 2016, compared with the same period in 2015.
Newport News revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2016, decreased $105 million, or 9%, from the same period in 2015.

factors.summary: The volume trends across the company's product lines show mixed performance, with some areas experiencing declines while others see increases.

evidence.text: Product sales at Ingalls decreased due to lower volumes in certain programs but increased in others. Newport News experienced lower volumes in some areas but higher in others.

Ingalls revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2016, decreased $16 million, or 3%, from the same period in 2015, primarily driven by lower revenues in Amphibious Assault Ships and the Legend-class NSC program, partially offset by higher revenues in Surface Combatants.

Aircraft Carriers revenues decreased primarily due to lower volumes on CVN-78 Gerald R. Ford and the execution contract for the CVN-72 USS Abraham Lincoln RCOH, partially offset by higher volumes on CVN-79 John F. Kennedy and the advance planning contract for the CVN-73 USS George Washington RCOH.
